qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization in cache backends permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL injection via band index parameter in PostGIS RasterField lookups. This finding describes pickle deserialization RCE in cache backends. Different vulnerability class (deserialization vs SQL injection), different root cause (unsafe pickle.loads() vs SQL injection in raster lookups), different affected components (cache backends vs RasterField).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-mwm9-4648-f68q) describes SQL injection via the band index parameter in Django's PostGIS RasterField lookups. The qualifying finding describes a pickle deserialization RCE vulnerability in cache backends, which is unrelated to the SQL injection vulnerability in RasterField.
evidence: Advisory describes SQL injection (CWE-89) in PostGIS RasterField band index parameter
Finding describes pickle deserialization RCE in cache backends (different vulnerability class, different root cause, different affected component)
Finding does not reference RasterField, PostGIS, raster lookups, or SQL injection in band index parameter
